question_text: A piece of dry ice (solid carbon dioxide) at -78°C is placed in an open container at room temperature (20°C). It disappears completely without melting into liquid first. The container shows no liquid CO₂ at any point. Explain this observation and name the process that is occurring.
correct_answer: The process is called sublimation. Dry ice has a very low temperature and a structure where carbon dioxide molecules are held together as a solid. At room temperature and normal atmospheric pressure, the heat energy is sufficient to cause the solid to change directly into a gas without passing through the liquid state. This happens because the conditions (temperature and pressure) are below the triple point of CO₂.
explanation: Sublimation occurs when a solid changes directly into a gas without becoming a liquid. For dry ice, the atmospheric pressure is too low for liquid CO₂ to exist, so the solid particles gain enough energy from room temperature to escape directly as gas molecules.
